# Test fixtures

These fixtures provide stable, minimal repositories for M1 parity testing:
the Rust `decon` dry-run output will be compared against the Python reference
crawl/scope/assessment baseline recorded here.

## Layout

| Fixture | Description | Module shape |
|---|---|---|
| `python-lib/` | Tiny single-package Python library | `src/` package + root config |
| `umbrella/` | Tiny Elixir-style umbrella monorepo | `apps/<name>/` (3 apps) + root config |
| `js-lib/` | Tiny TypeScript npm package | `src/` package + root config |

## Baseline

`baseline.json` was generated by running the Python reference tools against each
fixture:

- `utils.crawl_local_files.crawl_local_files`
- `utils.monorepo_scope.discover_modules` and `filter_files_by_scope`
- `utils.tutorial_quality.assess_setup_docs`

It records file counts, module inventory, filter statistics, and setup
documentation scores that M1 Rust tests should reproduce.

## Keeping fixtures stable

Do not add incidental files (editor configs, lockfiles, generated artifacts) to
fixtures. Every file here is intentional and contributes to the expected
baseline. If a fixture changes, regenerate `baseline.json` and update any
snapshot expectations.
